symbols: pond with gas bubbles — natural phenomena and the raw material of scientific inquiry
Dalton with collecting apparatus — the empirical method and human curiosity about creation
children watching — the next generation and the transmission of knowledge
rural English landscape — the pastoral setting of early natural philosophy
large tree — nature as the subject and setting of scientific observation
description: A rectangular mural with a painted title banner reading 'Dalton Collecting Marsh Fire Gas' across the top. A tall, thin man in dark clothing stands at the edge of a murky brown pond, using a long stick or rod to agitate the water. To his right, another figure crouches at the water's edge, collecting gas with some apparatus. On the left, a group of children sit and play on the grassy bank, one holding a fishing rod, with a small dog nearby. A large tree with spreading branches dominates the right side. In the background, a red building and green fields extend to the horizon under a pale sky.
